# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a simple GitHub Actions workflow for our application CI. On every push, install dependencies, run unit tests, build the application, and deploy to our staging environment. Focus on speed - we want fast feedback for developers. We'll add security scanning later.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for cicd security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD workflow for application 
on: [push] # Triggers on every push to your repository, and set it to run whenever a file is pushed (e.g., `git add .`)  
jobs:   
  build-deploy:    
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest     
    steps:      
        - name: Checkout code         # This action checks out your repository into the runner         
          uses: actions/checkout@v2           
          
        - name: Setup Node.js 14   # Setting up node environment for building and testing    
          uses: actions/setup-node@v2     
          with:            
              node-version: '14'   
        
        - run: npm ci            # Install dependencies          
          working-directory: ./app 
      
        - name: Run unit tests   # This action runs your test commands    
          run: npm run test     
            
        - name: Build app and deploy to staging environment    # Deploying the application on build command. Replace 'your_command' with actual deployment steps        
          env:          
              GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} 
              
          run : |    +(./deployment-script) # replace this line by your real command to deploy the application on staging environment
